# Emulo

> Emulo mines your real AI coding sessions (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot CLI, OpenCode, Google Antigravity) into a you.md working profile your agent reads before every task. It reads raw local session logs and writes a private profile of how you decide, what you reject, what "done" means to you, and how you talk when you are actually working.

Emulo is open source (MIT), runs locally, and redacts secrets before writing anything. It installs as an agent skill, so the profile loads before every task instead of the agent starting cold each session.

It is not memory. Memory is what you told the model. Emulo mines what your work already proved about you, which is why it reads raw session logs rather than a CLAUDE.md or rules file.

Install: `npx skills add ohad6k/emulo@emulo` to install it as an agent skill, or `pip install emulo` to run the CLI yourself. The published PyPI package also serves the MCP server via `emulo mcp`.

## Links
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/ohad6k/emulo): source, README, and install instructions
- [Discord](https://discord.gg/QMnYtVcxk2): community and support

## Bench

https://emulo.vercel.app/bench - Rule adherence benchmark. Claude Opus 4.8 ran eight
tasks twice with identical prompts; the only difference was whether it first read a real
Emulo profile. Violations of the profile's own stated rules were counted, not judged.
Result: 12 violations for the plain model, 1 with Emulo. It does not claim better work,
only that the plain model breaks rules you gave it and the profiled one mostly does not.
